text: The patient is positioned in the lithotomy position, with legs elevated and supported, providing optimal access to the rectal area for the surgical procedure.
The surgeon uses a lone star retractor to expose the renal area, providing a clear view of the anatomical structures in this region.
The prolapsed rectum is fully everted, revealing fibrotic tissues and a distal rectal stricture.
The surgeon makes a circumferential incision in the rectal wall using monopolar electrocautery, positioned approximately one centimeter distal from the dentate line.

The surgeon fully mobilizes the roll-up segment of the rectum using monopolar electrocautery, achieving hemostasis with bipolar I-IV when necessary, and ligates the mesorectal vessels with III-O Vicryl sutures.
The surgeon divides the rectal wall circumferentially proximal to the dentate line using surgical instruments, continuing the dissection process.
The surgeon divides the mesorectum at the rectal transduction line, using surgical instruments to separate the mesorectal tissue from the rectum.
The surgeon incises the anterior wall of the prolapsed rectal segment and places three full-thickness interrupted sutures using free-hand technique with Vicryl suture material.
The surgeon transects the distal part of the prolapsing rectum and performs a complete anastomosis using a suturing technique, ensuring the rectal continuity is restored.
